texto: When life is this dull, you have to invent purpose. Collecting torn-up newspaper gives you a hobby, provides an anchoring intimacy with your surroundings, keeps the streets clean. Or so you think. Then one day you wake up and realise that it was all a con: what you had thought was an escape from madness was in fact the arrival.
explicacao: Orientação direta de Alexander Masters sobre sentido da existência e experiência vivida. A afirmação “When life is this dull, you have to invent purpose. Collecting torn-up newspaper gives you a hobb…” funciona como lembrete prático ligado a reflexao, madness, invent, útil em momentos de reflexao.

texto: Put two macho groups together and give the first desperation and numbers, and the second truncheons and protective clothing, and the result is like a laboratory civil war.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Alexander Masters: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de temas ligados a reflexao. O trecho “Put two macho groups together and give the first desperation and numbers, and the second truncheo…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que reflexao, prison, macho pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de reflexao.

texto: If Stuart is a freak... it is because he has had the superhuman strength not to be defeated by this isolation. It is because he has had the almost unbelievable social adroitness to be able to fit in smoothly with an educated, soft-skinned person like myself and not make me frightened half to death. If Stuart's a freak, I salute freaks.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Alexander Masters: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de temas ligados a amizade. O trecho “If Stuart is a freak... it is because he has had the superhuman strength not to be defeated by th…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que amizade, freaks, homeless pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de luto e amizade.
